question.text: Meera Iyer, Compliance Officer of a listed entity, needs to file the corporate governance report for the quarter ending September 30, 2025. Who can sign this report under SEBI LODR Regulation 27(2)?

answer.text: Only the Chairman of the Board
Any independent director of the company
Either the Compliance Officer or the CEO
Only the Company Secretary if different from the Compliance Officer

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 27(2), the corporate governance report must be signed by either the Compliance Officer or the CEO. Since Meera is the Compliance Officer, she can sign it herself. Alternatively, the CEO can sign it. **The Trap:** Neither the Chairman nor independent directors are authorized signatories for this specific report. The restriction to CO or CEO ensures accountability from those most involved in governance implementation. **Cross-Exam Context:** The corporate governance report now includes related party transaction details and cyber security information, making the signatory accountability especially important. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In practice, companies often have the Compliance Officer sign the report as they are closest to the compliance data. The CEO option provides flexibility when the CO is unavailable. RTAs should verify signatory authorization when submitting data for these reports.
